title: usetparm
text: usetparm Set the value of a ProcScript function. Explain this code long usetparm(shortcode, unsigned char*value, unsigned char*param); Copy Parameters code Numeric code identifying the ProcScript function, as given in the table below. value Value, in string format, to be assigned to the ProcScript function. param Parameter to the ProcScript function. Specify zero or an empty string if the function has no parameter. Return Values 0 —Success. -1 —An error occurred, for example, the specified field or entity does not exist. Use Library: yrtl Description usetparm sets the ProcScript function identified by code to the value value . If the ProcScript function expects a numeric value, value should be the string representation of that value. If the ProcScript function requires a parameter (typically a field or entity name), that name should be passed in param in uppercase characters. The following table lists the ProcScript functions, in alphabetical order, that can be used with usetparm . These functions are a subset of those that can be accessed with usysparm . For a numerically sorted list of ProcScript functions, see the 3GL function usysparm tables. Table 1. Functions accessible via usetparm Function Code Param $batch 18 — $check 4004 — $disable 4003 — $fieldcheck 3003 { Field { . Entity }} $fieldproperties 3011 { Field { . Entity }} $fieldvalrep 3010 { Field { . Entity }} $formmod 1001 — $formtitle 1006 — $hide 4002 — $instancemod 7005 — $ioprint 3 — $keyboard 4 — $language 6 — $occcheck 2010 { Entity } $prompt 1004 — $properties 3009 { Field { . Entity }} $valrep 3008 { Field { . Entity }} $variation 5 — For more information on ProcScript functions, see the "ProcScript: Functions" topic. The following code uses usetparm to set the value of $ioprint to 255, and to set the $fieldcheck function TRUE for the field CODE . LEDGER. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void setstuff () { (void)usetparm(3,"255",0); /* Set $ioprint to 255 */ (void)usetparm(3003,"1","CODE.LEDGER"); /* Set $fieldcheck TRUE */ /* for field CODE.LEDGER */ } Copy Related Links ProcScript: Functions Refer to the topics under this section to understand functions related to ProcScript. usysparm Call a ProcScript function and return its value.
